project_identity.name: BuildContextMemory
project_identity.description: A lightweight system that preserves project memory and AI behavior consistency across sessions and tools.
project_identity.architecture_summary: Two-file system: AI_Starter.md defines AI behavior; Build-Context-Memory.json stores append-only project/session memory.

decisions_log.reason: Preserves historical decisions and prevents accidental context loss

sessions.last_session_summary: session_001: Project initialized with base AI starter and memory files; ready for foundation alignment work.
changes_made: Comprehensive codebase audit identifying foundation readiness (~60%)
Documentation alignment with PRD/DevPlan as highest-priority source
Complete README rewrite reflecting product vision and agentic architecture
Markdown conversion and structuring of all Initialize/ strategic documents
Tech stack dependencies declared in backend and frontend package.json
Agentic AI architecture framing in code and documentation
Build memory governance system established with mandatory update rules

file_module_map.purpose: Central implementation guide with agentic AI architecture, 14-week roadmap, and tech stack
Product requirements source of truth, structured with goals, pain points, user profiles
14-week development plan with tech stack, phases, milestones, and risk mitigations
Ignore Initialize/ folder to prevent committing project context
Added dependencies: @anthropic-ai/sdk, @sentry/node, redis
Added dependencies: @sentry/nextjs, @posthog/js
Updated comment to frame module as agentic boundary with planner, routing, memory, composition
Persistent build log with schema, template, and initial 4 entries documenting this session
Updated to mandate build memory maintenance for all human and AI builders

decisions_log.decision: Make PRD and DevPlan the highest-priority source documents
decisions_log.reason: Ensures all work aligns to product vision and prevents scope creep
decisions_log.alternatives_considered: Treating all docs as equal authority; leads to inconsistency

decisions_log.decision: Explicitly frame application as agentic AI in README and code comments
decisions_log.reason: Signals to all developers that AI planning, routing, memory belong in backend layers
decisions_log.alternatives_considered: Treating as standard AI-assisted app; loses architecture clarity

decisions_log.reason: Ensures traceability, accountability, and multi-session continuity for all builders
decisions_log.alternatives_considered: Optional logging; leads to incomplete context for future work

decisions_log.decision: Declare tech stack dependencies upfront in package.json
decisions_log.reason: Professional practice; enables npm install without manual dependency hunting
decisions_log.alternatives_considered: Add as needed during implementation; slows down and fragments setup

known_issues.issue: backend/src/config/env.ts has z.object syntax error from failed patch
.env example files not yet created (templates exist in documentation)
Dependencies declared but not installed (npm install not yet run)

errors_encountered.error: Patch failed to properly close z.object in backend/src/config/env.ts
errors_encountered.context: Tool limitations prevented auto-resolution of complex multi-line object closure
errors_encountered.resolution: Documented clean version in ALIGNMENT_SUMMARY.md for manual fix; users must copy-paste clean code

next_steps: Fix backend/src/config/env.ts using clean version from ALIGNMENT_SUMMARY.md

sessions.notes: Foundation is now ~95% ready for Phase 1 development. Only blockers are env.ts syntax fix and dependency installation. All strategic documents (PRD, DevPlan, README) are aligned and professionally formatted. Agentic architecture is explicitly communicated. Build memory system is active and ready for ongoing use.

sessions.last_session_summary: session_002: Foundation alignment completed with 9 major tasks. Blocker identified: env.ts syntax error from failed patch.
changes_made: Removed duplicate imports from env.ts
Fixed incomplete z.object closure (all properties now inside schema)
Removed orphaned properties floating outside object definition
Removed duplicate export statement
Added proper comments for each config section (Core, Database, Redis, AI Provider, CMS, Monitoring)

file_module_map.purpose: Environment variable validation schema using Zod with all required config sections

decisions_log.decision: Keep all configuration in single env schema
decisions_log.reason: Centralizes config validation; single source of truth for required vs optional vars
decisions_log.alternatives_considered: Split into multiple schema files; adds unnecessary complexity

errors_encountered.error: env.ts had 5 syntax errors from failed patch (duplicate imports, incomplete z.object, orphaned props, duplicate export)
errors_encountered.context: Previous patch merge failed to properly close z.object; properties scattered outside definition
errors_encountered.resolution: Replaced entire file with clean version; file now compiles without errors

sessions.last_session_summary: session_004: Fixed package.json JSON syntax. Both projects ready for npm install.
changes_made: Created backend/.env with environment variables (Supabase, Redis, AI keys, monitoring)
Created docker-compose.yml for containerized Redis development setup
Resolved @sentry/nextjs peer dependency conflict with Next.js 15 using --legacy-peer-deps
Verified all backend routes operational: /api/health, /api/recommendations, /api/roadmaps
Verified frontend pages rendering: home, /onboarding, /roadmaps

file_module_map.purpose: Environment configuration for backend services (Supabase, Redis, Claude API)
Local Redis container orchestration for development with healthcheck
Mock recommendation path discovery engine; agentic boundary established
Non-AI path signal matching; returns 3 recommendations with confidence scores
Curated roadmap library with 3 paths (Frontend, Data Analysis, UI/UX); one resource per concept
User onboarding multi-field form collecting background, interests, goals, time commitment
Displays all roadmaps with step-by-step nodes, explanations, and curated resource links

decisions_log.decision: Use --legacy-peer-deps for frontend npm install to unblock @sentry/nextjs with Next.js 15
decisions_log.reason: Quick development unblock; proper version alignment to be addressed in Phase 2
decisions_log.alternatives_considered: Downgrade Next.js or find compatible Sentry; adds unnecessary delay

decisions_log.decision: Skip Docker Redis startup in this session; defer to when Phase 1 actually needs caching
decisions_log.reason: Phase 1 doesn't require Redis; mock engine is stateless. Keeps startup simple.
decisions_log.alternatives_considered: Mandate Redis running; adds unnecessary infrastructure overhead for Phase 1

decisions_log.decision: Keep recommendation matching in backend (not AI service) for now
decisions_log.reason: Phase 1 design goal: mock engine to prove architecture before integrating Claude
decisions_log.alternatives_considered: Implement Claude immediately; adds cost and complexity prematurely

known_issues.workaround: Using --legacy-peer-deps flag; no functional impact in development

errors_encountered.error: ERESOLVE during frontend npm install: @sentry/nextjs@^8.0.0 has peer dependency next@^10.0.8 || \^14.0, but next@15.5.9 not compatible
errors_encountered.context: Next.js 15 is newer than Sentry version in package.json
errors_encountered.resolution: npm install --prefix frontend --legacy-peer-deps; full version alignment to be done in Phase 2/maintenance

validation_results: ✅ Backend health endpoint responds with HTTP 200: {"status": "ok", "service": "pathfinder-backend", "supabaseConfigured": false}
✅ Frontend landing page renders without errors
✅ Onboarding form loads and collects user input (background, interests, goal, time commitment)
✅ Backend returns 3 recommendations with confidence scores (65%, 65%, 55%)
✅ Recommendations page displays with reason text (e.g., 'This matches your signals around web')
✅ Roadmaps page loads all 3 paths: Frontend Development, Data Analysis, UI/UX Design
✅ Each roadmap displays 3 steps with explanations and one external resource link per step
✅ All external resource links are valid and open in new tab
phase_1_deliverables: ✅ User onboarding form component (collects background, interests, goals, time commitment)
✅ Mock recommendation engine returning top 3 paths with confidence + reasoning
✅ Curated roadmap library with 3 beginner career paths
✅ One-resource-per-concept design enforced in UI and data model
✅ Backend API contracts defined: POST /recommendations, GET /roadmaps, GET /roadmaps/:slug
✅ Agentic boundary established at backend ai-engine service layer (mock for Phase 1; ready for Claude in Phase 2+)
✅ Full end-to-end user flow from landing → onboarding → recommendations → roadmaps
✅ Responsive design with Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui components
✅ Foundation for Phase 2 AI core work (Claude integration, conversational flow)
next_steps: Commit Phase 1 code to git with message: 'Phase 1 complete: MVP with mock recommendation engine and 3 curated roadmaps'
Deploy staging environment to Vercel (frontend) + Railway/Render (backend) for team review
Begin Phase 2: Integration with Claude API for conversational path discovery
Add user authentication (Supabase Auth or Auth0) for persisting user data
Build roadmap progress tracking system (mark nodes complete, streak counter)
Expand roadmap library with field-of-study recommendations (Business, Engineering, Arts, etc.)
Add news/opportunities feed integration
Implement 'Go Deeper' toggle for optional extra resources per concept
sessions.notes: Phase 1 is 100% complete and fully functional. The MVP demonstrates core product value: personalized path discovery + one curated resource per concept. Backend architecture is properly layered with agentic boundary at ai-engine service level. Ready for Phase 2 AI integration (Claude) and Phase 3 secondary features (progress tracking, news feed, reskilling mode). All Phase 1 deliverables from DevPlan (Week 1-4) have been achieved. The team can now move forward with AI core work.
